title: Single-step purification by heat shock
author: Hossein Tarrahimofrad

steps_raw.instruction: Cultivation cells 50 mL 37 °C Overnight , collected by centrifugation 3500 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes and resuspended in 4 ml lysis buffer ( 50 millimolar (mM), NaH2PO4 , 300 millimolar (mM), NaCl , 10 millimolar (mM), Imidazole and 0.05 % volume, Tween 20 at pH 7 )
The mixture was sonicated with 4 × pulses 00:00:40, Amper 0.7 followed by 00:00:20 rest between cycles at 4 °C .
The crude extracts were then centrifuged 9000 x g, 4 °C, and the resulting supernatant was heat-treated in a hot water bath at 90 °C 00:15:00 .
Insoluble material was separated by centrifugation at 13000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes , and the supernatant, containing the enzyme, was analyzed on a 12% SDS–polyacrylamide.
The purified elution fractions were dialysed Overnight 50 millimolar (mM) pH 7 4 °C .
